summary: Default the close→merge step to opening a PR instead of merging local main
description:
Today /todo-implement step 8 does:
  git checkout main
  git merge --no-ff todo/<id>
  git branch -d todo/<id>

That skips human review entirely. This session shipped at least two
things to local main that a PR would have caught (false-positive bug
adbb0d8d; --force parent close 3d9717e2).

Goal: make 'open a PR and stop' the default; keep local-merge available
but not the default.

Shape:
1. New 'todo pr' command that:
   - Verifies HEAD is on todo/<id>
   - Pushes branch to origin (gh pr create handles -u)
   - Auto-generates PR title from the ticket summary
   - Auto-generates PR body from description + resolution notes
     (for parents, aggregate children's resolution notes)
   - Runs 'gh pr create' (or 'gh pr edit' if one already exists)
   - Prints the PR URL on success
   - Errors clearly if gh missing or no remote configured

2. /todo-implement step 8 rewritten to call 'todo pr' instead of merging.
   Local merge stays documented as an opt-out path (e.g., 'when working
   solo and main isn't protected, you can skip the PR step and merge
   locally'). No --no-pr flag — just two paths in the skill.

Out of scope: auto-merge after CI. PRs are review gates; agents stop
after opening them.
